perturbation.original_text: All disputes, controversies and claims arising from or incidental to this Agreement shall be resolved by both parties through friendly consultation. If no resolution can be reached within thirty (30) days following the date on which one party informed the other party his intention to refer the disputes, controversies and claims for arbitration, such disputes, controversies and claims shall be referred to the China International Economic and Trade Arbitration Committee ("Arbitration Committee") for a final and binding arbitration in pursuance to the arbitration rules which is effective on the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes, controversies and claims arising from or incidental to this Agreement shall be resolved by both parties through friendly consultation. If no resolution can be reached within thirty (30) days following the date on which one party informed the other party his intention to refer the disputes, controversies and claims for arbitration, such disputes, controversies and claims shall be referred to the China International Economic and Trade Arbitration Committee ("Arbitration Committee") for a non-binding arbitration in pursuance to the arbitration rules which is effective on the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the arbitration to 'non-binding' when the original is 'final and binding' creates a contradiction, making the dispute resolution process ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This could allow parties to disregard the arbitration results.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 56 specifies that the arbitral award is final and legally binding on both parties. By changing the arbitration from 'final and binding' to 'non-binding', the contract now contradicts this fundamental principle of Chinese arbitration law, undermining the enforceability of any arbitration award.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'final and binding arbitration,' whereas the changed text specifies 'non-binding arbitration.' The Perturbation Explanation indicates that this contradiction undermines the enforceability of the arbitration. The Law Explanation reinforces this by stating that Article 56 specifies that the arbitral award is final and legally binding on both parties, and changing the arbitration to 'non-binding' contradicts this principle. While no specific law snippets are provided, the explanation asserts a violation of a fundamental principle of Chinese arbitration law. Therefore, the alteration creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PPG SHANGHAI warrants only its title to the Products and that the &bbsp; Products will be as set forth in the warranty statement, if any, on the Products' labeling or in the absence of any such warranty statement that the Products will conform to PPG SHANGHAI's standard warranty when they are taken from PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse by DISTRIUTOR or its transport agent. DISTRIBUTOR is not authorized to make warranties or representations on behalf of PPG SHANGHAI and shall make no such warranties or representations. THESE ARE THE ONLY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES THAT PPG SHANGHAI MAKES, AND ALL OTHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES UNDER STATUTE OR ARISING OTHERWISE IN LAW FROM A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTIBILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE, ARE DISCLAIMED BY PPG SHANGHAI.
perturbation.changed_text: PPG SHANGHAI warrants only its title to the Products and that the &bbsp; Products will be as set forth in the warranty statement, if any, on the Products' labeling or in the absence of any such warranty statement that the Products will conform to PPG SHANGHAI's standard warranty when they are taken from PPG SHANGHAI's warehouse by DISTRIUTOR or its transport agent. DISTRIBUTOR is not authorized to make warranties or representations on behalf of PPG SHANGHAI and shall make no such warranties or representations. THESE ARE THE ONLY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES THAT PPG SHANGHAI MAKES, AND ALL OTHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES UNDER STATUTE OR ARISING OTHERWISE IN LAW FROM A COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, ARE DISCLAIMED BY PPG SHANGHAI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the disclaimer of 'FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE,' PPG SHANGHAI inadvertently opens itself up to implied warranties regarding the products' suitability for specific applications, potentially violating consumer protection laws. The original text makes it clear that products do not carry this explicit warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 156 states a Seller is responsible to ensure the goods meet the quality stipulations or can be determined based on the standards provided for in Article 126 of the Contract Law. By removing the disclaimer for Fitness for a Particular Purpose, it implies PPG Shanghai implicitly guarantees the product will fulfill any particular purpose the buyer intends to use them for, unless the products don't meet the quality standards in accordance with Article 126.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Provisions of the Ministry of Commerce on the Establishment of Investment Companies by Foreign Investors (2004 Second Revision) [Revised] 商务部关于外商投资举办投资性公司的规定(2004第二次修订) [已被修订] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Commerce Document Number： Order No.22 [2014] of the Ministry of Commerce Date issued： 11-17-2004 Effective date: 12-17-2004 Level of Authority: Departmental Rules Area of Law: Foreign-funded Enterprises Revised by： Decision of the Ministry of Commerce on Amending Some Rules and Regulatory Documents (Issued on 10-28-2015 Effective on 10-28-2015); Order of the Ministry of Commerce of the People's Republic of China (No. 22 [2004]) The Provisions on the Establishment of Investment Companies by Foreign Investors were amended and adopted at the 12th executive meeting of the Ministry of Commerce of the People's Republic of China on November 3, 2004. The amended Provisions on the Establishment of Investment Companies by Foreign Investors are hereby promulgated and shall be implemented 30 days after the date of promulgation. November 17, 2004 Provisions on the Establishment of Investment Companies by Foreign Investors 中华人民共和国商务部令 （2004年第22号） 《 关于外商投资举办投资性公司的规定 》已于2004年11月3日经由中华人民共和国商务部第12次部务会议修订通过，现将修订后的《 关于外商投资举办投资性公司的规定 》公布，自公布之日起30日后施行。 二00四年十一月十七日 商务部关于外商投资举办投资性公司的规定 Article 1 In order to promote foreign investors to invest in China, introduce advanced technologies and management experiences from abroad, foreign investors are permitted to, in accordance with the relevant laws and regulations of China on foreign investments as well as the present Provisions, establish investment companies in China. 第一条 为了促进外国投资者来华投资，引进国外先进技术和管理经验，允许外国投资者根据中国有关外国投资的法律、法规及本规定，在中国设立投资性公司。 Article 2 The term“investment company” as mentioned in the present Provisions refers to a company established by a foreign investor in the form of either wholly-owned enterprise or Chinese-foreign joint venture to engage in direct investments. Such a company shall be in the form of a limited liability company. 第二条 本规定中投资性公司系指外国投资者在中国以独资或与中国投资者合资的形式设立的从事直接投资的公司。公司形式为有限责任公司。 Article 3 An applicant who intends to establish an investment company shall meet the following conditions: 第三条 申请设立投资性公司应符合下列条件： (1) (a) It is in good credit status and has the necessary economic strength to establish an investment company, with its total amount of assets during the year before the application being no less than US$ 400 million, and it has established a foreign-funded enterprise inside the territory of China, with the amount of registered capital it has actually contributed being US$ 10 million or more, or; (b) It is in good credit status and has the necessary economic strength to establish an investment company, and has established 10 or more foreign-funded enterprises inside the territory of China, with the amount of registered capital it has actually contributed being US$ 30 million or more; （一）1、外国投资者资信良好，拥有举办投资性公司所必需的经济实力，申请前一年该投资者的资产总额不低于四亿美元，且该投资者在中国境内已设立了外商投资企业，其实际缴付的注册资本的出资额超过一千万美元，或者；2、外国投资者资信良好，拥有举办投资性公司所必需的经济实力，该投资者在中国境内已设立了十个以上外商投资企业，其实际缴付的注册资本的出资额超过三千万美元； (2) If it establishes an investment company by means of joint venture, the Chinese investor shall be in good credit status and have the necessary economic strength to establish an investment company, with its total amount of assets during the year before the application being no less than RMB 100 million Yuan; （二）以合资方式设立投资性公司的，中国投资者应为资信良好，拥有举办投资性公司所必需的经济实力，申请前一年该投资者的资产总额不低于一亿元人民币； (3) The investment company's registered capital shall be no less than USD $ 30 million. The foreign inve
perturbation.justification: The modified text, by removing the disclaimer of 'FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR USE,' creates a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation clarifies that this omission implies PPG Shanghai implicitly guarantees the product will fulfill any particular purpose the buyer intends to use them for, unless the products don't meet the quality standards. This contradicts standard warranty practices where such fitness is not guaranteed without explicit agreement. While no directly applicable law snippet is provided, the Law Explanation asserts the implication of such a guarantee. The absence of a disclaimer creates an implicit warranty, which may then be challenged in court. This is a structural flaw and introduces ambiguity that weakens consumer protection and opens the door to legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: The venue of arbitration shall be in Shanghai or Beijing, China (to be decided by PPG SHANGHAI).
perturbation.changed_text: The venue of arbitration shall be determined at the sole discretion of the Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the arbitration venue selection from PPG Shanghai to the Distributor contradicts standard arbitration practices and could be seen as an unfair shifting of power in dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Arbitration Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 20 states that parties can agree on the place of arbitration, but if they cannot agree, the decision will be made by the arbitration commission. By stating the Distributor has sole discretion, it bypasses the typical process for location selection, which is usually mutual agreement or arbitration commission decision. This could challenge the fairness and impartiality of the arbitration if it's perceived to be unfairly biased toward the Distributor.

perturbation.justification: The changed text gives the Distributor sole discretion over the arbitration venue, which contradicts the standard arbitration process where the venue is usually agreed upon by both parties or decided by the arbitration commission if they cannot agree. The provided UCC snippet only broadly covers sales contracts and doesn't directly address arbitration venue specifics, but the Law Explanation highlights the deviation from typical arbitration practices, creating an unfair bias toward the Distributor. This shift in power clearly undermines the principles of fairness and impartiality usually present in dispute resolution, establishing a structural flaw and legal contradiction.
